commit.message:
fix: enable cross-reference scoring + wire AQI into predictions

Cross-reference scores were always N/A because the DB schema was missing
5 columns (teleconnection_alignment, ensemble_regime_score,
extreme_value_score, external_forecast_agreement, forecast_convergence)
that the INSERT statement referenced — causing silent failures.

Changes:
1. Added 5 missing columns to cross_reference_scores CREATE TABLE
2. Added migration lines for existing DBs
3. Updated INSERT to include external_forecast_agreement + forecast_convergence
4. Changed cross-ref job to run immediately on startup (was next_run_time=None)
5. Changed error logging from debug to warning for visibility
6. Wired air_quality_aqi from get_recent_aqi() into predictor pipeline

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Drawdown : corriger off-by-one, tréso = risque lissé, retrait réel affiché

Trois corrections au calcul du drawdown en mode "croquer capital" :

1. Off-by-one : l'annuité était calculée sur computeCapitalProjection(annees)
   qui fait N contributions, mais la boucle n'en fait que N-1 (y=0 est le cas
   spécial, y=annees est déjà en phase 2). Le pool se vidait 2 ans trop tôt.
   Fix : base = computeCapitalProjection(annees-1), PV avant première croissance.

2. Tréso sécurité = risque lissé : dans un modèle sans aléas, la tréso est
   consommée implicitement chaque année (elle absorbe les chocs). Elle
   s'amortit donc linéairement sur la période de drawdown, et n'est PAS
   dans le pool de retrait actif.

3. drawdownMois affiché = retrait réel (plafonné au pool disponible), pas
   la cible théorique. Plus de revenus fantômes quand le pool est vide.

Résultat : le pool hors PER se vide pile à 63 ans, le PER prend le relais
à 64 sans trou.

Fix Auth.tsx redirecting new users to /setup/factory instead of /subscription

without a factory directly to /setup/factory, bypassing both Index.tsx
and the subscription flow. Now all no-factory redirects go to /subscription.

commit.message:
Initial release — Soul AI v1.2.1

Soul is a standalone AI agent (like Claude Code) that runs in your terminal.
308 MCP tools, 31 core engines, multi-turn conversation with session persistence,
message queue + debounce, smart history windowing, and works with Ollama (free)
or any LLM API.

Features:
- CLI agent: type `soul` to chat, `soul-setup` to configure
- MCP server: works with Claude Code, Cursor, Gemini CLI
- HTTP API + Web UI: 3D neural network visualization
- Brain Hub: export/import knowledge between Soul instances
- Soul Family: spawn child agents that collaborate

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: soul
repository.description: Soul AI — Your Personal AI Companion. Local-first, private, loyal. 308 MCP tools. Works with Ollama (free) or any LLM API.

commit.message:
Initial commit: Recursion Visualizer

A web application to visualize recursive function execution.

Features:
- Recursive functions: Fibonacci, Factorial, Hanoi Tower
- Call stack visualization with push/pop animations
- Recursion tree visualization using Canvas 2D
- Execution control: step, auto-play, speed adjustment
- Memoization comparison and call count display

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix: move writable files to %APPDATA% to support Program Files installation

sqlite3.OperationalError on installed EXE was caused by app trying to
create interxdb.db in C:\Program Files\ (read-only). All writable files
(interxdb.db, interxdb_config.json, logs) now go to
%APPDATA%\InterXDB_FFServer\ which is always user-writable.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Feature/sessions share (#1)

* Add sessions-share/receive feature for sharing Claude Code /resume sessions

Implements a 2-phase interactive protocol (LIST_SESSIONS / SELECT_SESSION)
over the existing encrypted transport layer (LAN mDNS + WebSocket relay).
Receivers can browse session metadata and download selected .jsonl files
to resume in their own environment.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Build sessions catalog from raw .jsonl files instead of sessions-index.json

sessions-index.json doesn't always exist. Added build_sessions_catalog.py
to scan .jsonl files directly and extract metadata (first prompt, message
count, timestamps). Updated SKILL.md to use this script.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix relay mode: handle reconnection and multi-request per connection

The relay room was dying after the first receiver interaction (list sessions),
causing the second request (select session) to fail with "room not found".

- Server: wrap relay connection in reconnect loop, handle_peer_ws now loops
  to support multiple requests (list → select) in a single peer connection
- Receiver: add relay_list_and_select() to do list+select in one connection,
  refactor auth into relay_connect_and_auth() helper

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Fix relay mode: use single persistent connection instead of reconnect loop

The reconnect-on-disconnect pattern caused the relay to assign a new room ID,
making the original room unreachable for receivers. Now uses a single persistent
websocket connection matching the working distill serve.py pattern. Also handles
PEER_DISCONNECTED messages gracefully inside handle_peer_ws.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Bump websocket max_size to 50MB for large session payloads

The default websockets library max_size (1MB) is too small for
encrypted+hex-encoded session .jsonl files. Set max_size=50MB
on all websockets.connect() calls to match SESSION_MAX_SIZE.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add auto-shutdown and --keep-open option for serve_sessions.py

Server now automatically shuts down after the first session download
by default. Use --keep-open <minutes> to keep the server open for a
specified duration, allowing multiple receivers to connect.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add detailed session sharing guide to README

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: 修复换链接任务执行后未更新 next_swap_at 导致任务停止的问题

问题描述：
- 换链接任务执行一次后就停止，不再继续执行
- 影响所有时间间隔的任务（5分钟、30分钟、60分钟、120分钟、360分钟）
- 生产环境中 26/27 个任务受影响

根本原因：
在以下3种情况下，任务执行后没有更新 next_swap_at 字段：
1. 手动模式（方式二）：URL未变化时
2. 自动模式（方式一）：URL未变化时
3. 任务失败：连续失败次数 < 3 时（保持enabled状态）

修复内容：
1. updateTaskStats 函数：添加 next_swap_at 计算和更新逻辑
2. setTaskError 函数：添加 next_swap_at 计算和更新逻辑
3. updateTaskAfterManualAdvance 函数：添加 next_swap_at 计算和更新逻辑

影响范围：
- src/lib/queue/executors/url-swap-executor.ts
- src/lib/url-swap.ts
